01 — About

Senior UXUI Designer with 5+ years turning complex workflows into intuitive, scalable digital products.

My background spans Gamification, Pharma, and Fintech — industries where engagement, trust, and clarity aren't nice-to-haves, they're the product.

At Globant I work end-to-end — UX flow analysis, high-fidelity UI, Design Systems, and QA — on products used by millions. I integrate AI tools (Claude, Gemini) to accelerate research synthesis and explore directions faster.

I hold a Postgraduate in UX Research and a Graphic Design degree — which means I can argue both the why and the how.

03 — Case Studies

Banreservas · Fintech 5 min read

Designing for Two Currencies: When Business Logic Meets UX Reality

How I structured a multi-currency payment flow for a major Caribbean bank — with no brief, no research, and a client with strong opinions.

Context

Users with multi-currency credit cards had to complete two separate transactions to pay their PESOS and USD balances. Double the steps, double the friction, double the chances of abandonment.

Key decisions

  • Unified both currencies into a single payment flow with transparent breakdown
  • Ran a self-initiated competitive benchmark before designing any screen
  • Owned UX copy end-to-end — no UX writer on the team
  • Designed under ambiguity: no formal brief, no research phase

Takeaway

Constraint is a design material. When there's no research phase, you create your own inputs — benchmarks, logic mapping, copy iteration — and that discipline shows in the outcome.

Read full case study on Medium →
BAC Credomatic · Fintech 5 min read

From MVP to 2.0: Designing a Banking Login That Users Actually Trust

A visual designer's perspective on iterating a high-stakes authentication flow across 6 countries.

Context

BAC Credomatic's login was functional but not trusted. As the product scaled to 4M+ users across 6 countries, the entry point needed to evolve — from an MVP that worked to a 2.0 that felt right.

Key decisions

  • Biometric-first hierarchy — Face ID and Touch ID as the primary entry point
  • User's name as a trust signal before authentication
  • Red-only color decision to reinforce brand and reduce cognitive noise
  • Pre-login quick actions to reduce abandonment before users even sign in

Takeaway

Login is not just an access gate — it's a trust moment. Every visual decision, from color to hierarchy to copy, either builds or erodes that trust in the first 3 seconds.

Read full case study on Medium → View on Behance →
Wellness App · HealthTech 7 min read

Designing for the Arab World: What I Learned Localizing a Health App for Oman

Beyond flipping the layout — how real research with users, Apple experts, and a translation agency changed how I think about cross-cultural design.

Context

Expanding a gamified wellness platform to Oman meant more than RTL support. It meant understanding a culture, a work week, a visual language, and a set of values that couldn't be solved by mirroring the layout.

Key decisions

  • Reviewed every icon to determine which should mirror and which shouldn't
  • Arabic typography required larger sizing — more visual detail per character
  • Calendar week start adapted to Sunday–Thursday work week in Oman
  • Illustrations reviewed for cultural appropriateness with a native Arabic speaker

Takeaway

Cultural research is not optional in localization. Talking to a native Arabic speaker before designing a single screen was the most important decision in the project.

Read full case study on Medium → View on Behance →
